data.body:
PASS 1 review — criterion E4 (assumption/premise verification). I probed every codebase assertion in the plan via Read/Grep rather than relying on cached knowledge.

AFFIRMATIONS (assertions verified true against source):
- "`apply_outbound.py:416,441`" — VERIFIED: `is_not_found(exc)` at apply_outbound.py:416 and `getattr(exc, "status_code", None) in (404, 410, 403)` at :441, both inside `except JiraAPIError as exc:` blocks.
- "`batch_dispatch.py:77,307`" — VERIFIED: `exc.status_code` 4xx fast-fail at batch_dispatch.py:77 (in `except JiraAPIError`), and `http_status(exc)` at :307 (`_is_illegal_transition_400`).
- "the `_engine/rebar_reconciler/_errors.py` helpers already exist" — VERIFIED: `_errors.py:6 http_status(...)` and `:22 is_not_found(...)` are present and implemented.
- "`reconcile.py:1155` `except BaseException ... raise`" — VERIFIED verbatim: reconcile.py:1155 `except BaseException as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — must re-raise after recording` then `raise` at :1157.
- "Migrate stderr-diagnostic prints (`reconcile.py`, `__main__.py`, etc.)" — VERIFIED such prints exist (reconcile.py has ~20 print sites; __main__.py has stderr `ERROR:`/`OK:` prints), and __main__.py also emits stdout data output (report at :162, json result at :254), corroborating the "keep acli/jira data output semantics intact" caveat.

FINDINGS:

- [ ] [E4] AC4 asserts "This package removed from the allowlist", but the ONLY current allowlist entry for this package is a `T201` per-file-ignore that pyproject.toml documents as a PERMANENT legitimate-print surface, not a removable transitional entry — verify which allowlist AC4 means and correct the wording.
  criteria: [E4]
  location: Acceptance Criteria line 4 ("This package removed from the allowlist") vs pyproject.toml:202-232
  evidence:
    - pyproject.toml:226-231 (the only per-file-ignore touching this package's BLE001 history) reads: "reconciler subprocess — BLE001 SWEPT by 0569 ... KEEPS T201: the reconciler is a subprocess whose stderr ... + stdout ... prints ARE its operational output — a legitimate-print surface."
    - pyproject.toml:232 `"src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/*" = ["T201"]` — a KEPT T201 ignore, with no BLE001 entry present for this package. There is no remaining BLE001 allowlist line to remove; the T201 line is explicitly NOT slated for removal.
    - The plan body's own caveats ("Migrate stderr-diagnostic prints ... keep any acli/jira data output semantics intact"; rollback/cleanup swallows "stay intentionally broad") are consistent with T201 staying — so "removed from the allowlist" is ambiguous/likely inaccurate as written.
  suggested_fix: Reword AC4 to name the specific removal precisely — e.g. "the BLE001 ignore for this package is removed (no BLE001 per-file-ignore line for src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/ remains); the T201 entry at pyproject.toml:232 stays as a documented legitimate-print surface" — so the criterion is verifiable.

- [ ] [E4] The "add characterization tests FIRST" premise treats the safety net as not-yet-existing, but `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_errors_classification.py` already pins `is_not_found`/`http_status` and is explicitly tagged to this ticket — clarify whether the AC is "author new" or "confirm/extend existing".
  criteria: [E4]
  location: "What" bullet 1 + AC1 ("Characterization tests cover ... before remediation") vs tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_errors_classification.py:1-15
  evidence:
    - test_errors_classification.py:1-8 docstring: "Characterization tests for the reconciler's HTTP error-classification helpers. Pins the CURRENT behavior of `http_status` / `is_not_found` BEFORE the error-handling sweep (epic ring-gun-jot, ticket 0569) touches the broad `except` sites..." — i.e. this file is named as 0569's own safety net.
    - test_errors_classification.py:15 `from rebar_reconciler._errors import http_status, is_not_found` and helper exception classes (`_StatusCodeExc`, `_CodeExc`, `_BothExc`) exercise both `.status_code` and `.code` paths — the classification is already characterized.
  suggested_fix: State in the plan whether AC1 is satisfied by extending the existing test_errors_classification.py (and to what coverage delta) or by adding new tests, so a reviewer can verify the "FIRST" ordering claim against an already-present file rather than assuming it is absent.

Note: both findings are clarity/verifiability of stated premises, not blockers — the underlying remediation scope is well-grounded and the file/line references all check out. (Separate from the G4 logging-substrate findings already on this ticket, which I did not re-litigate.)
